Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Confirm the order is deliverable and schedule correctly.
Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Verify the home, hookups, access path, and specs match the install.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Build a feasible route and verify the correct product, parts, and tools are loaded.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Confirm the actual site matches the plan and that safe access exists.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Remove the old unit safely when haul-away is included.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Place and connect the new unit correctly and to code.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Verify the unit works, brief the customer, and capture sign-off.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Resolve defects, damage, incomplete work, or customer complaints quickly.

Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Wants clear timing and confidence the order and install scope are correct.
Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Must provide photos, site details, and readiness info so the crew can finish.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Usually unaware unless timing shifts; mainly waiting for the promised service window.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Must be present, provide access, and learn whether the job can proceed.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Wants safe removal without damage or haul-away surprises.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Watches install quality and wants the work to be safe, correct, and clean.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Wants proof the unit works before the crew leaves.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Wants fast ownership, clarity, and a remedy timeline.

Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Scheduling; operations management steps in when order data conflicts with install reality.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Dispatch / route planning; operations management handles major conflicts or shortages.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Crew lead; dispatch or operations joins if site conditions conflict with the plan.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Crew lead; management joins if damage, safety, or scope disputes appear.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Installer or crew lead; technical support or management joins for code, safety, or product issues.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Crew lead; dispatch or operations joins for disputes, damage, or failures.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Customer care, field service, or operations management depending on severity and cost.

Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Warehouse/load team; pre-install support; crew lead.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Customer; dispatch; technical support; operations management.

Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Order data conflicts with install reality.
Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Site readiness or specs do not match what is needed to install.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Product, part, or route assumptions are wrong at load/dispatch time.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Actual access path or site conditions do not match readiness information.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Shutoff access, utility condition, or haul-away scope is not what the crew expected.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: A code, safety, product, or utility issue appears during hookup.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: The unit fails test or the customer disputes quality, damage, or completeness.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Severity or ownership is classified incorrectly after the visit.

Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Whether the conflict is minor and correctable or a true serviceability problem.
Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Whether the gap can be fixed remotely before dispatch or makes same-day install impossible.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Whether the shortage or route problem can be absorbed with resequencing/substitution or forces a replan.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Whether the mismatch is still safe/manageable or is a true no-go.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Whether the condition is safe and within crew authority or requires escalation.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Whether the issue is remediable within field authority or forces escalation/revisit.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Whether the problem is fixable immediately or requires handoff and evidence-backed escalation.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: How severe and costly the issue is, and which team actually owns the remedy.

Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Bad appointments; failed installs; wasted route capacity; customer distrust.
Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Crew arrives to a non-installable site; failed delivery; route waste; reschedule; lower trust.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Failed stop; wasted crew time; late route; customer disappointment; route disruption.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Injury; property damage; failed stop; route disruption.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Damage; leak; unsafe condition; failed install; complaint.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Unsafe install; liability; callback; customer harm; incomplete job.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Repeat visit; complaint escalation; chargeback/dispute risk; unresolved quality issue.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Delayed remedy; extra touches; duplicated work; angry customer; avoidable cost.

Stage 1: Order Review & Appointment Conf: Order data creates ambiguity about what can actually be delivered or installed.
Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Safety, code, or cost decisions exceed field/front-line authority.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Conflicts or shortages exceed dispatch authority.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Crew cannot safely proceed because site conditions do not match the order.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Damage, safety, or scope dispute exceeds crew authority.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Code, safety, or product issues appear.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Dispute, damage, or failure cannot be resolved within the stop.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Severity or cost exceeds front-line authority.

Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Prep checklist, readiness requests, and timing changes if exceptions arise.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Mostly internal; route and crew prep.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Usually no automated notification during install.
Stage 7: Testing & Customer Sign-Off: Completion message, POD, photos, and customer briefing.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Follow-up, case updates, and resolution timeline.

Stage 2: Pre-Delivery Qualification & Si: Photos; notes; readiness checklist; specs; utility requirements.
Stage 3: Route Planning & Inventory/Load: Dispatch dashboard; route planning tools; inventory/load checks.
Stage 4: Arrival & Home Access Confirmat: Order packet; site notes; customer contact details.
Stage 5: Appliance Removal: Haul-away request; safety checklist; disposal rules.
Stage 6: Install & Utility Connection: Product specs; install kit; tools; code requirements.
Stage 8: Post-Install Issue Resolution: Case notes; complaint details; reason codes; service history.
